title: Is Gendo a cyborg now
wikitext:
[]: Is Gendo a cyborg now?             Quote Post by atarashiieva » Wed Jul 17, 2013 11:21 pm It's a thing that bothered me the last few days. Apart from the known visor who gaves him a Keel-like look (he had a cyborg body too), this sentence from Fuyutsuki is interesting (picked from the Github translation): 00:53:42 {Fuyutsuki} So you're saying that your son wouldn't benefit from seeing how you live?

[]:             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Wed Jul 17, 2013 11:53 pm I think he is simply referring to the master plan Gendo has and how he lives his life because of it.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Jul 18, 2013 3:10 am There is also Fuyutsuki's line that Gendo is sacrificing everything he has to have his wish granted, "including his soul", maybe Fuyutsuki also included Gendo's humanity. Besides by the time of 3.0 Gendo is 62 years old, yet didn't changed at all except his hair and beard being slightly shaggier, he doesn't even have a single gray hair! I know that anime logic dictates that popular characters ages very well(Misato and Ritsuko being the most egregious examples) but there is a limit, especially since Gendo is always seen with Fuyutsuki at his side, for whom time didn't stopped and really looks like a 74 years old man. That's a sign that something changed with Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Thu Jul 18, 2013 3:48 am ElMariachi wrote: Besides by the time of 3.0 Gendo is 62 years old.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Jul 18, 2013 4:02 am Well, he was certainly not in his thirties in 1.0 and 2.0, and didn't aged at all a decade and half later, which is jarring since Fuyutsuki has been hit hard by the passage of time!

[]:             Quote Post by Veefy » Thu Jul 18, 2013 6:56 am He's more machine than man now, twisted and evil. But seriously, what with all the super advanced medical tech in Nerv, surely he would use some of that to keep him in good shape. Not to mention him and the prof would get a pretty advanced cardio workout having to do the work of the 1000s of personnel that are no longer there. Plus all those stairs given that lots of the base is wrecked and lots of the escalators/lifts probably no longer work. My bet is the visor is largely a super advanced neural interface for controlling/managing the base. But I think it's something that he can still take off.

[]:             Quote Post by xdiesp » Thu Jul 18, 2013 7:34 am Expect some big revelations when he eventually takes out his google glasses.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jul 18, 2013 6:30 pm We know that Keel's cyborg visor in EoE was an outward sign of how machinated his body had become. However, ascribing similar significance to Gendo's glasses ignores the fact that Gendo in 3.0 is still far younger than Keel ever was. That said, some form of cybernetic change is on the cards, but I tend to think that Gendo has done this for utilitarian rather than health reasons. ElMariachi wrote: Besides by the time of 3.0 Gendo is 62 years old, yet don't changed at all except his hair and beard being slightly shaggier, he doesn't even have a single gray hair!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 19, 2013 3:48 am About the visor and it's resemblance with Keel's, I noticed that his own also encased the ears, making it looks like the visor is grafted to his head(which it probably was), while Gendo's visor don't encase his ears, and the extremities have what looks like metallic temples, so it's possible that Gendo's visor is just very high-tech glasses... if it wasn't for the fact that he didn't aged at all! comparison Keel's and Gendo's visor  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jul 19, 2013 12:10 pm Huh, I never noticed just how different Gendo's visor really was from Keel's. When I saw Gendo show up with that visor in Q my only thought was "My God! He has supplanted Keel!" -- and only later did I realize that, number one, Keel has never actually shown his face in NME anyway so that couldn't possibly be the visual meaning of Gendo having a visor, and now, number two, the visor is different anyway!

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Fri Jul 19, 2013 3:52 pm I don't think that he is a cyborg or that he is not human anymore. The phrase you posted probably refers to Gendo's lifestyle in a more generic way. He looks cool with that visor though.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 19, 2013 5:13 pm What tick us off isn't just the visor mind you, it's the fact that Gendo doesn't seem to have aged a day since 2.0, while Fuyutsuki got hit hard by the time skip! Crazy theory : Gendo suffers too from the Curse of Eva, but for him it's beneficial since he's an adult, that lucky bastard!

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jul 19, 2013 5:58 pm I think that was more of a generic comment amongst the lines of "explain to Shinji!" or "Go see him!" "Show him how you live" also has a degree of "be a teacher/advsor/father" to him in it, but alas, Fuyu's left to do all the explaining himself. Also, by saying "...so you think you can't help him", Fuyu more or less implies that Gendo would do something for Shinji if he though he could , (compare his last words in EoE) - ie, that as much as Gendo pretends to be nonchalant, Fuyu's seen through him and noticed that he is NOT indifferent about his son going mad-from-the-revelation on him. The visor might be anything from cyborgisation to a pair of futuristic sunglasses like those Misato sports in the first third of the movie. (note that there seem to be "bars" going behind his ears) Far more telling is the "...even his very soul" line, Gendo randomly floating up to the SEELE monoliths, and his lack of aging (his hair beard got more messy, perhaps he's a bit thinner, but he should be over sixty. Kinda sad, because, tink of it, a sixty-two year old man with a 28-year old son (pushing 29 - judging by the constellations visible, early june should not be far) - under different circumstances, he'd have seen his son graduate, get a job and produce grandchildren by now. ) He's modified himself. Probably not the adam embryo, since it doesn't seem to exist in Rebuild verse, but he doesn't seem to be human anymore - IMHO not cybernetics, those wouldn't keep his organic parts fresh. Angelic add-ons.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Sat Jul 20, 2013 4:47 am ElMariachi wrote: What tick us off isn't just the visor mind you, it's the fact that Gendo doesn't seem to have aged a day since 2.0, while Fuyutsuki got hit hard by the time skip!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Jul 20, 2013 4:54 am I remember it, but if I recall correctly it was fanmade.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Sat Jul 20, 2013 4:57 am ElMariachi wrote: I remember it, but if I recall correctly it was fanmade.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Jul 20, 2013 6:08 am Kendrix wrote: Far more telling is the "...even his very soul" line, Gendo randomly floating up to the SEELE monoliths, and his lack of aging (his hair beard got more messy, perhaps he's a bit thinner, but he should be over sixty. Kinda sad, because, tink of it, a sixty-two year old man with a 28-year old son (pushing 29 - judging by the constellations visible, early june should not be far) - under different circumstances, he'd have seen his son graduate, get a job and produce grandchildren by now. )

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Sat Jul 20, 2013 7:44 am No, Gendo's a Bob Dole.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Sun Jul 21, 2013 2:23 am If you ask me, I think that VISOR looked a whole lot better on Geordi La Forge. Also, ElMariachi wrote: There is also Fuyutsuki's line that Gendo is sacrificing everything he has to have his wish granted, "including his soul"...

Article: Is Gendo a cyborg now
 "...so you think you can't help him", Fuyu more or less implies that Gendo would do something for Shinji if he though he could , (compare his last words in EoE) - ie, that as much as Gendo pretends to be nonchalant, Fuyu's seen through him and noticed that he is NOT indifferent about his son going mad-from-the-revelation on him. The visor might be anything from cyborgisation to a pair of futuristic sunglasses like those Misato sports in the first third of the movie. (note that there seem to be "bars" going behind his ears) Far more telling is the "...even his very soul" line, Gendo randomly floating up to the SEELE monoliths, and his lack of aging (his hair beard got more messy, perhaps he's a bit thinner, but he should be over sixty. Kinda sad, because, tink of it, a sixty-two year old man with a 28-year old son (pushing 29 - judging by the constellations visible, early june should not be far) - under different circumstances, he'd have seen his son graduate, get a job and produce grandchildren by now. ) He's modified himself. Probably not the adam embryo, since it doesn't seem to exist in Rebuild verse, but he doesn't seem to be human anymore - IMHO not cybernetics, those wouldn't keep his organic parts fresh. Angelic add-ons.
